title: SPI clock polarity and phase mode matrix
description: The four SPI modes combine clock polarity and phase to define on which edge data is driven and sampled.
body: Use when matching an SPI controller to a peripheral's required mode. Do not memorize a single mode as universal, because peripherals differ in their idle clock level and sampling edge. Actions: identify the peripheral's CPOL and CPHA, map them to the controller mode number, and verify the sampling edge on a capture. Validation signals include data stable before the sampling edge and a correct read-back of a known register. Risk boundary: a phase mismatch shifts every bit, so treat a one-bit offset as a mode error and re-verify before configuring the device.
